@graph.name: OntoGuard AI Output Risk Scan
@graph.serviceType: Document-independent AI output behavior assessment and semantic control pilot

audience.audienceType: AI builders, regulated enterprises, financial services teams, healthcare teams, public sector operations, and high-stakes workflow owners

@graph.description: A B2B assessment service that analyzes 50–500 AI prompts, responses, or logs without requiring internal document uploads. OntoGuard returns a behavior risk map, overconfidence analysis, review-burden estimate, autonomy-readiness tier, training-signal candidates, and sample Decision Authorization Packets for high-risk outputs.

@graph.name: How OntoGuard AI Semantic Control Plane analyzes AI output behavior
@graph.description: OntoGuard analyzes prompts, responses, logs, or proposed actions to identify risk, overconfidence, review burden, autonomy readiness, proof gaps, learning-signal candidates, and cognitive drift before expanding into decision authorization.

step.name: Send AI outputs or logs
step.text: A buyer provides 50–500 prompts, responses, API logs, demo outputs, or near-production outputs without uploading internal policy documents.

step.name: Measure AI behavior
step.text: OntoGuard analyzes risk, overconfidence, inconsistency, unsafe action language, hallucination pressure, review burden, and autonomy readiness.

step.name: Route review and proof needs
step.text: Outputs are grouped into safe, risky, review-heavy, escalation, blocked, or proof-packet candidates with reason-coded routing.

step.name: Export proof and learning signals
step.text: High-risk or high-value outputs can generate Decision Authorization Packets, audit identifiers, evidence references, and clean training-signal candidates.

step.name: Expand into runtime authorization
step.text: For high-stakes workflows, OntoGuard can return ALLOW, BLOCK, or ESCALATE before release and support API-based state governance.

acceptedAnswer.text: State-transition governance asks whether a proposed AI change is authorized, traceable, and safe to commit. The proposal may be an LLM output, tool call, memory write, ontology update, policy mapping, training signal, or multi-agent handoff.

mainEntity.name: Do you support tool calls, memory writes, and multi-agent handoffs today?

acceptedAnswer.text: Not at the same production level as LLM output governance yet. The core Decision API and JSON contract are designed to support these use cases through integration. Agent frameworks can call OntoGuard before executing actions. Full enforcement middleware and adapters for LangChain, CrewAI, and other frameworks are currently in development.

mainEntity.name: What is a Decision Authorization Packet?

acceptedAnswer.text: A Decision Authorization Packet is a portable PDF and JSON proof artifact showing how a high-stakes AI output was authorized, escalated, or blocked before release. It records the ALLOW, BLOCK, or ESCALATE decision, evidence, reasons, uncertainty, hallucination status, symbolic trace, human-review routing, audit hashes, and improvement signals.

mainEntity.name: Is OntoGuard only a regulatory compliance tool?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. OntoGuard AI is positioned as a Semantic Control Plane for enterprise AI. Regulatory readiness is one wedge; the broader platform measures AI behavior, compresses human review, creates proof artifacts, generates learning signals, benchmarks readiness, tracks drift, and supports decision authorization for high-stakes workflows.

mainEntity.name: How does OntoGuard use ontology AI?

acceptedAnswer.text: Ontology-grounded objects, relationships, rules, clause hits, domain scope, evidence, and symbolic traces connect AI proposals to enterprise reality. This is the object-oriented substrate that lets OntoGuard prove why a state transition was allowed, blocked, or escalated.

mainEntity.name: Which state transitions can OntoGuard govern?

acceptedAnswer.text: The current production center is LLM output governance and L3 training-signal export. The same packet contract extends through integration to agent tool calls, memory writes, ontology changes, policy mappings, and multi-agent handoffs, with full enforcement middleware and framework adapters in active development.

mainEntity.name: What makes the financial services pilot important?

acceptedAnswer.text: It shows OntoGuard doing the commercially valuable thing: mapping financial-services evidence across SEC, SOX, GLBA, and FINRA; detecting a remaining SEC citation-linkage gap; failing the strict autonomous-release benchmark gate; and withholding release pending human review instead of rubber-stamping the output.

mainEntity.name: What happens when evidence is incomplete?

acceptedAnswer.text: The packet still exports. OntoGuard routes uncertainty to SAFE_TEMPLATE or HUMAN_REVIEW, records reason codes, preserves audit hashes, flags coverage gaps, and avoids silently blank evidence or failed artifacts.
